summary: Tag each Agricola card with the mechanics/features it uses

cards.note: Build a free 1x1-pasture stable on play; restricted geometry (exactly 1-space pasture); passing minor. Stable is a piece from supply, no resource cost.
Randomly select and play an occupation free from hand; random draw during play; passing. Play-a-card grant is exotic.
Immediate wood per room (+1 if rooms>people); plain state read; passing minor.
Immediate goods conditional on owning specific majors; plain state read; passing.
Immediate food/grain per grain/veg field; plain state read; passing.
Immediately gain 1 grain + 1 veg; prereq is a play condition only; passing.
Room for one person; play only via Major Improvement action; disables future renovation (capability-off).
Gain 1 boar on play; unplanted field tiles hold 1 boar each (new holders tied to farm geometry/fields).
Gain 3 clay then free renovate (declinable, discount not deferrable); once via on-play.
Each time you build 2+ rooms at once, discount on the build cost; fires on the build-rooms sub-action.
After using a wood accumulation space, if >=7 wood, optionally build 1 stable for 1 wood; keyed on space type.
After next time you take animals and fully accommodate them, may plow 1 field; one-shot latch on that event, needs a pending/uses flag.
Once per game, using Farmland/Cultivation with first person of round, plow 2 extra fields; needs first-of-round detection + used flag.
On play, plow up to 2 fields; alt/extra food cost depending on round (E-FOODCOST for the conditional +1 food play cost).
Each time you build rooms (with rooms>people): grant a Family Growth sub-action + 1 food; capped 1 growth per build. Fires on the build-rooms sub-action; growth granted so T-AFTER/optional.

cards.new_category: PLACE-FROM-SUPPLY: temporarily place an extra person from personal supply on a scheduled future round, returned home after.
cards.note: On play marks a future round space; in that round you place a person from supply (extra worker without normal gain), returned in returning-home. Extra placement in a future round — closest to E-EXTRAPLACE but scheduled/self-supplied; flagged new_category.
Immediately after using a Quarry space, you get a Major-or-Minor Improvement action requiring >=1 stone spent; mandatory grant of the improvement action.
Each work phase place another person on the first non-accumulating space used by any player if only 1 person there; extra placement using an occupied-ish specific tracked space.
On play: +2 wood and may immediately build Clay/Stone Oven major at a reduced fixed cost of 1 clay + 1 stone.
Lessons spaces considered unoccupied (use occupied space); replace each food an occupation costs with wood (currency substitution on play-cost).
Each returning-home phase may pay 1 grain for 1 bonus point; if you do, each other player gets 1 food. Optional round-end hook.
Each Bake Bread action: may exchange 1 grain for 2 food + 1 bonus point (must bake normally). Optional sub-action hook with a scoring bonus.
End-game bonus: 1 point per major improvement, capped at unused farmyard spaces. Cost 2 food is normal play cost.
Each time you build fences: may exchange 1 wood + 1 fence-from-supply for 2 food + 1 bonus point. Fence piece consumed.
Returning-home: if returning a person from Fishing, 2 bonus points per newborn returned. Needs to know worker was on Fishing and newborn status.
On play: exchange any number of food for 1 bonus point each, up to completed-harvests count. One-time conversion into score.
Each time you renovate: may pay 1 wood for 1 bonus point + 1 grain. Optional renovate sub-action hook.
Card becomes a shared action space; a user gets 3 bonus points, another player must first pay you 1 grain to use it.

On play places clay on each unused space (goods-on-card stack); each time a space becomes used you get that clay and may convert 1 clay->2 food. Turning a space used = using an action/sub-action; treat as own-action hook, optional exchange.
Fires when you upgrade Fireplace to Cooking Hearth (a build/major action), mandatory +2 wood +1 veg.
Each time you use Fishing/Forest space you also get 1 wood/food; mandatory.
Each time you build 1+ stables in one turn (on-turn only per clarification), place 1 food on next 3 round spaces.
Fires once when you first no longer live in a wooden house; schedules food on next 6 round spaces.
Each time you use Sheep Market, place 1 food on next 2 round spaces.
On play, schedule 1 food on next N round spaces = number of fences built (reads farm fence count).
After obtaining wood: with 5+ wood may convert 1 wood->3 food, with 7+ must. Optional-then-mandatory threshold; needs event provenance to fire per wood-obtain.

Fires when 1 reed is placed on a non-empty Reed Bank during preparation (the round refill/reveal accumulation). Ambiguous seam: it's the preparation-phase good-placement on accumulation spaces, closest to S-REVEAL/S-SOR; not clearly modeled.
Each time you use Fishing space you get an additional 2 food.
Each time you use a wood accumulation space while Pig Market has >=1 boar, also get 2 food; reads another space's goods.
On play get 5 food; at end of each non-harvest round must pay 1 food or take a begging marker. Recurring end-of-round mandatory food cost.
On play, food based on sheep/cattle counts (plain conditional state read).
In returning-home phase of rounds 8/10/12 may take 1 veg from a veg field (crop manipulation), exchange for 3 food +1 bonus point. Round-triggered optional; grants a bonus VP.
After harvesting veg, if 3+ veg optional convert 1 veg->6 food; with 4+ mandatory. Fires after field-phase harvest -> T-AFTER/S-HFIELD; the mandatory-at-threshold path is F-MANDCHOICE.
Play-cost is returning Fireplace/Cooking Hearth (E-RETURNCOMP); at-any-time good->food conversions and a bake-bread grain->2food rate (E-BAKESPEC).
After field phase of each harvest, optionally use a baking improvement to turn exactly 1 grain into food (not a Bake Bread action).
During feeding phase optionally exchange grain for bonus points + 3 food; the bonus-point rule is E-SCORE.
CORRECTION: printed cost is alt (4 Clay/2 Stone)=E-ALTCOST. In field phase get 1 food per grain field harvested -> auto goods keyed on field phase.
Each time before an unconditional Sow action, get 1 grain. Keyed on the sow sub-action, before -> T-BEFORE/S-SUB, automatic gain.
Each time you use an animal accumulation space while already owning that animal type, get 1 grain. Space-keyed automatic gain.
Each time you build fences >= current round number, immediately get 1 veg. Keyed on the build-fence sub-action; needs round-number comparison but effect is plain gain.
At end of each round without a harvest, optionally move 1 veg from a field to supply. Round-end hook, moves crop off a field -> E-CROPMANIP.
Each time you use a Quarry space, add 1 good to each single-crop planted field -> increases crops on fields, E-CROPMANIP.
After turning 2+ unused spaces into used in one action (plow/build), get an additional Sow action -> granted sub-action, optional.
Each time you build 1+ stables on your turn, place 1 wood on next 3 round spaces, collected at round start -> E-SCHED. Off-turn stables excluded.
At start of each work phase, if have 1+ clay, optionally exchange 1 grain for 2 clay + 1 food. Start-of-round-keyed optional conversion.
On play get 1 clay (ONPLAY). Each time ANY player uses Pig Market space, get 2 clay -> space hook firing on own+opponent actions.
Fires on your Sow sub-action planting veg -> S-SUB, mandatory gain.
Using resource accumulation spaces stacks goods on card; released at start of specific rounds.
After using any action space, optionally take 1 building resource from a space with >=4.
Breed outside harvest in returning-home phase w/o following harvest; pay 1 grain even from a field.
On play grants immediate plow; passing minor.
Plain conditional goods gain based on clay count; passing.
Gain 1 cattle (must be accommodated); sheep is the play cost; passing.
Each pasture holds 2 more animals (existing slot capacity boost).
On play +1 clay; passive: clay substitutes for wood on fences.
Places field tile on round+5 space; at that round start, optionally plow it.
Using Farmland/Cultivation grants additional Bake Bread sub-action.
Use an occupied Wish for Children space (if only one other person there).
End-game bonus points scaled by farmyard spaces pastures cover.
On play: immediate bonus points + food per remaining round (bonus points from text -> E-SCORE).
End-game bonus points scaled by house material; house-material bonus cards are mutually-exclusive-scoring type.
Places food on next 3 round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.
Fires each time any player uses Cattle Market; you get 3 food, others get 1 (transfer to opponents).
Returning-home phase; conditional on gross building-resource gains during the work phase, needing tracked provenance/counter.
Each time after building an improvement, get 1 food.
After using a wood accumulation space, may exchange 2 wood for 3 food, returning the wood to the space.
Bake Bread in a round following a harvest gives +3 food; requires knowing round-relative-to-harvest position.
Each time you use Grain Seeds, +1 grain.
Places vegetables on future round spaces (rounds +4/+7/+9).
At any time, move a crop from a planted field to an empty field.
Every improvement costs 1 wood less.
Each time you use Fishing, +1 food and +1 reed.
Each time you use a stone accumulation space, +1 stone.
After fencing a new undivided pasture of >=4 spaces, get 2 sheep on it; depends on pasture geometry.
On play, mandatory-fence a free new pasture; free fence pieces; adjacency to existing pasture is farm geometry. Passing not in code list.
On play +1 veg (effectively grain->veg exchange); passing minor.
Card itself holds a person (not a room); creates a new person holder.
Continuous cost change: wooden rooms cost 2 wood + 2 reed.
On play +1 food; each renovate grants optional free stable build (no wood).
Place 2 field tiles on card; twice per game using Farmland grants an extra plow from the card's stack (counter).
Place two people in succession if one uses an animal-market space; extra placement gated on which space.
On play +1 food; each occupation played grants an extra Bake Bread action.
On play +1 bonus pt per remaining complete round; then continuous no-renovate restriction.
Play cost scales with person count; extra clay+food is just a cost read from state, no lasting mechanic.
Field phase: food per sheep thresholds; end-game bonus point per 3 sheep.
On play place 1 food on next 3 round spaces, collected at those rounds' start.
Each Fishing use places food on next 3 round spaces (future scheduling).
Field phase: food per 3 sheep + per 2 cattle.
Each time you use one of 4 specific action spaces (board-position dependent) -> before-use hook granting food.
Start of each round, conditional on wooden house, get 1 food.
Start of field phase, if grain+veg+empty field, get 3 food.
Using Grain Seeds while Farmland occupied -> 3 food; occupancy of a specific board space.
Place grain on future round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.
Card is a field that grows only vegetables.
Place wood on remaining even-numbered round spaces (board numbering); collected at round start.
Each time you use Day Laborer, also get 3 clay.
At any time, exchange clay for stone at a rate.
Place wild boar on round spaces, collected at round start; animals must be accommodated.
Get 5 clay + a Renovation action (must pay cost); passing minor.
Randomly select an occupation from hand; play it for 2 food (grants a play-occupation action) or give to left player.
Immediately get wood equal to people on accumulation spaces; passing. Plain state-read count.
Immediate goods based on plain count of occupations left in hand; passing.
On play, gain stone = people count (plain state read); passing minor.
On play, gain food scaled by bottom-row majors (plain state read); passing.
On play, choose get 1 reed OR convert 1 reed->1 cattle; passing.
Card holds 1 animal per pasture (new holder); after each breeding phase gain food per empty spot.
Card itself holds up to 3 same-type animals; not a pasture.
Places a stone room on round 12; at start of round, free build if stone house else discard. Conditional room build at future round.
After using a wood space, use only that wood to build exactly 1 pasture with one free fence; needs the taken-wood provenance.
Each time you use a wood space, pay 2 wood to plow 1 field, paid wood returns to space.
Place a field on a future round space (offset by building resources); at start of round may plow it.
Place fields on rounds 7-9; at start of each may plow the field.
4 food on card; each time you obtain grain get 1 food from card; when empty grants family growth even without room.
Gain 2 food, place a temporary person (per errata mandatory) removed next returning-home; never reach 5. Temp worker classification.
Take round-14 action space card face-up; only you can use it until placed on board. Exclusive private early access to a round space.
Each time you use Grain Utilization, may take a Build Fences action instead of one of its two sub-actions.
After a turn in which you build a room/stable/fence, you MAY build a specified major improvement (paid normally); off-turn stables excluded.
After using Forest, may return 2 wood there to play an occupation for free (a granted play-occupation action gated on returning goods).
Bonus point each turn you use a Lesson space AND a cooking improvement same turn; needs tracking what was used that turn.

cards.new_category: PALISADE: place 2 wood on an edge fence-space that functions as a fence and scores a bonus point; can exceed 15 fences.
cards.note: Instead of a fence piece, place 2 wood on an edge fence space as a palisade-fence worth 1 bonus point.
Scoring: +2 bonus points if >=2 orthogonally adjacent unused farm spaces exist (geometry-dependent optimization).
At any time exchange 1/3/5 grain for 3/4/5 food plus 0/1/2 bonus points.
Next time you take animals from an accumulation space and fully accommodate them, +1 bonus point each; one-shot on that obtain event.
Once ever, when sheep count first reaches the threshold, immediately +2 bonus points.
On play, +1 food; prereq is a play-condition, not a mechanic.

cards.new_category: SPACE-LOCKOUT: forbids using certain farmyard spaces (adjacent to house) until all others are used — a self-imposed placement/usage restriction.
cards.note: Blocks using house-adjacent unused spaces until all other spaces used; no existing code captures a farm-space-usage restriction, hence unclear.
Each time you use Fishing or Reed Bank, also get 1 grain and 1 wood.
On play, alternate 2 wood / 1 wild boar on next 4 round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.
Becomes a shared action space: user exchanges wood for wood+food; other players pay you 1 food toll to use it.
Each time you use Grain/Vegetable Seed, place 1 food on each of the next 3/2 round spaces, collected at those rounds' start.
On play, adds rounds 3 and 4 to current round and schedules 2 food on those future round spaces (collected at round start); adding to current round is board-position dependent.
On play, places food/stone on the next several round spaces, collected at the start of those rounds.
Places 2 food on card at play; wood-space use moves food off the card to supply, stone-space use adds food to the card — a goods stack kept on the card, mandatory each use.
After each improvement/occupation is placed in front of you, if counts of improvements and occupations are equal you get 2 food; needs to compare running counts.
Each time you use a clay accumulation space, get food equal to your wild boar count; plain state-read reward on a space hook.
Play restriction is a state-computable prerequisite; effect is plain immediate food equal to current round.
At the end of each non-harvest round, optionally convert wood->2 food or stone->4 food; fires at round end but is an optional exchange.
Immediate 2 food on play; after each unconditional Sow sub-action, get 1 food per stable.
Places 3 food on card at play; wood-space use draws 1 food off card; renovating restocks card to 3 (restock also a sub-action hook on renovate — S-SUB). Goods stack on card.
Immediate 1 food on play; +1 food per vegetable taken from a field during the harvest field phase.
Immediate food; amount (4 vs 2) depends on whether played on the Major Improvement action space — where the card was played.
Each Fishing use, optionally pay 1 grain to schedule 1 food on each of the next 6 round spaces; grain cost is a plain resource cost, optional.
Each Lessons use, before paying occupation cost, optionally exchange 1 grain for 4 food.
Each Grain Utilization use while Fishing is occupied, get +2 food; depends on occupancy state of another board space.
On play, place grain on next 2/3/4 round spaces collected at round start; alt-cost determines count but same effect scaled, so E-SCHED not E-ALTCOST fork.
Before each Bake Bread sub-action, get grain per person on accumulation spaces; clarification makes bake mandatory so F-AUTO.
At any time pay 4 clay + 2 food to place veg on next 2 round spaces; food cost + scheduled crops.
Buy crops before start of each round ending with harvest — timed buy (food->crop conversion); ambiguous whether ATWILL or a start-of-round hook, tagging both convert and optional buy.
On play, conditional plain goods based on a state read (harvests == occupations played).

cards.new_category: SOW-IN-PASTURE: pastures may double as fields (sow in them), reducing their animal capacity
cards.note: Lets you sow in 1-2 cell pastures which then count as fields and hold fewer animals — dual field/pasture status; no single existing code captures the pasture-as-field conversion.
On play, plain goods conditional on room count (plain state read).
Before playing/building an improvement, get 1 wood usable to pay for it — effectively reduces improvement cost.
On play schedules 5 wood on round spaces; next renovate removes remaining promised wood (take-back hook on renovate sub-action).
On play, place reed on remaining rounds 5/8/10/12 spaces collected at round start.
Each time any player takes >=3 stone from an accumulation space, you get 1 stone; fires on own or opponent action, needs amount/provenance.
Before each work phase, optionally take 1 resource from a heavily-stocked accumulation space; timing tagged as start-of-round/before-work; depends on board goods state.
After each harvest, optionally buy one good with food (conversion); tagged after-feed as the closest harvest seam; optional so F-TRIG.
On play build a stack of goods (incl. animals) on the card; at any time pay 1 clay to take the top good — per-card consumable stack; animals must be accommodated when taken.
On play, immediately build 1 stable (granted sub-action) using a stable piece from supply; passing minor.
If played in work phase, immediately place another person (additional placement); passing minor.
Places stone on empty fields, harvested next field phase; manipulates field contents/rule, needs per-field state; passing.
The card itself holds up to 1 of each animal type (new holder); not a pasture per clarification.
Continuous cost change: renovate/build-room no longer requires reed.
Grants an immediate Build Fences action with free fences next to field tiles (geometry-dependent discount).
Places 4 field tiles on card (stack); each Farmland use, may plow up to 2 fields from card (granted plow, before space use).
If last space used in work phase is an accumulation space, immediately after take a Bake Bread action; needs to know last space used.
On play +2 food; each Farmland/Cultivation use may also take a Bake Bread action.
Lets three majors be built via Minor Improvement action (extends eligibility) and reduces their cost by 1 stone.
Scoring bonus: 1 point per stone room; mutually exclusive with other stone-house scoring cards.
Scoring: -1 point per card left in hand; the no-discard restriction is a play/state rule but the mechanic is a text scoring rule.
From now on each new field tile placed costs 1 food; must be able to pay before placing.
After each harvest's feeding phase, may exchange 1 food for 2 different building resources or 1 vegetable.
After using Vegetable Seeds space, may place another person on an accumulation space for +1 good; optional extra placement.
Places food on next 8 round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.
Opponent using Fishing pays you 1 food (transfer from opp), then 2 food placed on Fishing that round; needs to track which rounds used.
Building stables gives food (last one 3 food, needs to know which is last); may pay 1 clay instead of wood for one stable (cost substitution).
In feeding phase may convert 1 veg to 5 food (once per feeding); bonus point rule for 5th/6th vegetable.
Places grain on rounds 8/10/12 spaces, collected at start of those rounds.
After playing an occupation, get 1 vegetable.
Card is a veg-only field; harvested veg can immediately convert to 4 food each.
Places wood on remaining even round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.
Places stone on remaining even round spaces, collected at start of those rounds.
On play in round 3/6/9/12, get scaling food; passing minor.
Immediately get 1 boar + 1 cattle (animals need accommodation); passing minor.
In returning-home phase, if more rooms than people, may pay 1 food for a Family Growth action; once per round.
Get 1 food each feeding phase; can no longer hold animals in house (capacity override).
Each time you build a clay room, may pay an alternate cost formula -> HOOK before build-room sub-action, cost modifier.
On play place 2 field tiles on card; each use of Farmland/Cultivation may plow up to 2 fields from the card -> granted plow sub-action, field tiles stacked on card.
Each use of Lessons may play 1 additional occupation for 2 food -> grants a play-a-card sub-action at a food cost.
End-game bonus points for unfenced stables each holding exactly 1 of a type; requires optimizing arrangement across stables.
Play restriction computable from state (rounds left vs unused farmyard spaces); otherwise only the plain printed VP.
Field phase of each harvest: gain food scaled by cattle count; plus end-game bonus point per 2 cattle.
Each use of a wood accumulation space, if own >=1 boar, may pay 1 food for 1 bonus point -> hook on space, food cost, scoring gain.
Alternate food and sheep on each remaining round space, collected at round start -> scheduled food + scheduled animals needing accommodation.
After playing each occupation this game, gain an escalating good; needs a running count of occupations played (1 grain -> field). Field gain is a goods gain.
Place 0/1/2/3 food on next 4 round spaces, collected at round start -> scheduled food.
Place 1 food on next round spaces up to sheep count (plain state read at play time), collected at round start -> scheduled food.
Immediately before each occupation play (even before paying cost), get 3 food -> mandatory before-play hook.
On play get 6 food; may no longer use rounds 12-14 action spaces -> gains goods and revokes access to future action spaces.
Before each Bake Bread action, may exchange 1 clay for 1 grain (and must then bake) -> before bake sub-action hook, conversion, once per action.
Adds rounds and places veg on those round spaces (E-SCHED); at start of those rounds you may buy the veg for 1 food (deferrable convert). Adding to current round = board manipulation.
"Each time after you use Fishing" -> T-AFTER on a space; optional buy grain/veg for food = optional convert.

cards.new_category: WOODFIELD: a field that grows wood (a non-crop resource) via the sow/harvest mechanics, holding a stack on the card that counts as 1 field but plants as 2.
cards.note: Card is itself a field (sow/harvest wood on it); wood-as-crop is a novel field type not captured by grain/veg field codes.
Place reed on next 3 round spaces, collected automatically at start of those rounds.
At end of rounds 8/10/12 you get stone per veg-field and wood per veg in supply; mandatory, keyed on end-of-round, counts from plain state.
On play get 1 sheep (animals to accommodate). In each feeding phase optionally exchange 1 veg for 1 animal of a type you have -> gaining animals in feeding phase, breed-adjacent, optional convert.

cards.new_category: REBUILDFENCES: raze all existing fences and rebuild them (returning placed pieces then re-placing, adding up to N), keeping animals.
cards.note: Razes and rebuilds fences (component return + re-place), adds up to 3, counts as building fences; passing minor. Not standard grant-build.
Immediately gain wood per occupation (plain state read); passing minor.
Immediately gain clay per clay room + major improvement (plain state read); passing.
Choose 3 food OR food per sheep (keep sheep); a choice over plain goods gain; passing.
Add 1 good to each field holding exactly 1 good -> manipulates crops on fields (not plain loose goods); passing.
Buy sheep/boar/cattle for 0/1/2 food if accommodatable -> animals to accommodate + convert food to animal; passing.
"Once you have 4 rooms, house holds 5 people" -> level-triggered one-shot latch raising person capacity.
Card holds 1 cattle per pasture -> a new animal-holder slot on the card whose capacity scales with pastures.
First renovation only, if 5+ wood rooms: renovate wood->stone directly (extends renovation target) with a 2-stone discount (cost modifier). One-shot on first renovation -> latch.
Before using Pig Market space, optionally take a Build Fences action (pay wood as usual).
On play, grant an optional plow of 1 field not needing adjacency (extends plow legality).
At any time, discard all goods from a planted field (crop manipulation as cost) to plow 1 field.
Each time you use Farmland/Cultivation, optionally plow 1 additional field.
Each time a Quarry space is revealed, if room, optionally take Family Growth without placing a person.

cards.new_category: CARD-HOLDS-PERSON: the card becomes a holder to which a placed person (and newborn) is moved.
cards.note: On play move the first person placed this phase to this card (needs place log), then optionally place another person. E-CAPNEW-like holder; also E-EXTRAPLACE + worker manip.
Use Day Laborer + adjacent Lessons with one person in order; both then occupied. Two spaces with one person (worker manip); adjacency = board geometry.
At start of the NEXT harvest only, get a Family Growth if room. One-shot next harvest.
Each time you use Major Improvement/House Redevelopment, optionally also play 1 occupation at cost 1 food (grants the play-occupation action).

At end of field phase each harvest, optionally pay 1 grain for 2 bonus points; if so all others get 1 food. Repeated bonus-point rule + gift to opponents. (Fires at end of field phase -> after field, use S-HFIELD/T-AFTER.)
During scoring, bonus points equal to total negative points (max 7) — end-game bonus-point rule computed over your own negatives.
Play restriction computable from state (no player has 5+ cards); no ongoing effect stated beyond plain printed VP. Pure prerequisite/condition -> NONE.
During scoring, tiered bonus points for count of unplanted fields — end-game bonus rule reading field state.
Immediately after each harvest -> after-hook at harvest boundary; optional exchange of 1 reed for a bonus point.
Immediately after each time you use a clay accumulation space -> after-hook on space; optional exchange 1 clay for bonus point, clay returned to the space.
On play, all other players gain 1 food each -> gift transfer to opponents.
Each time you use Traveling Players you also get 1 bonus point (mandatory); the card becomes an accumulation space in 1-3p (per clarification) but effect here is a +VP hook on the space.
Play cost forks with reward: pay grain->get vegetable / pay reed->get wood.
Each time you build a major improvement -> place food on next 3 round spaces, collected at round start.
Each time you use Day Laborer, also schedule 1 food on next 4 round spaces.
Once per round: pay 1 veg for a bonus point; if done, schedule food on next 2 round spaces.
On play, schedule 1 food on remaining round spaces up to 3x planted fields (depends on farmyard field state).
After each turn in which you newly use at least one unused farmyard space, get 1 food; needs tracking whether a farmyard space became used that turn.
In feeding phase, per empty unfenced stable, optionally exchange 1 grain for 5 food; depends on stable count/state.
On play get 1 food per completed round left; at-will exchange 1 sheep + 1 boar for 1 cattle (animal conversion needs accommodation).
For each new wild boar obtained from an action space, also get 1 food; keyed on obtaining an animal from a space source.
Each time you use a cooking improvement to turn 2 goods into food at once, get 1 additional food; keyed on the cook/convert sub-action.
After field phase (optional): trade 1 grain + 1 fence piece for 5 food; fence is a game-piece cost.
Feeding-phase optional conversion of exactly 1 wood/clay/stone into food.
On play buy 1 veg for 3 food; at-will discard veg onto a field's veg (crop manip) for 4 food.
After using a wood accumulation space, if <=5 wood, get 1 food; checked-before-after-cards ordering.
On play +5 food (and return oven); before each bake-bread, may build Clay/Stone Oven major; oven can't bake.
Each bake-bread action, once: 1 grain -> 2 food + 1 bonus point; must bake normally too.
Each harvest, use each cooking improvement once to double food from 1 animal/veg.
Feeding phase: spend 1 grain from supply + 1 grain from a field for 2 bonus pts + 4 food.
Once per round pay 1 grain to schedule 1 food on each of next 4 round spaces, collected at start.
After each harvest, if 2 grain get 1 food, if 3+ get 1 grain (errata OR); mandatory. Fires after harvest end.
Start of each non-harvest round, if >=1 sheep in a pasture, get 1 grain; needs to know round is non-harvest.
At any time exchange 3 grain in a field for 1 veg in that field (crop-on-field manipulation).
Breeding phase: if newborns of >=2 types, also get a Sow action (granted sub-action, optional).
On play immediately do a field-phase harvest for yourself only, then get a Major/Minor Improvement action.
After each unconditional Sow sub-action you get 1 grain (or veg from r11); mandatory 'you get'.
Accrues 1 wood/round on card (returning-home phase), then before building a baking improvement move up to 4 wood to supply — a stacked-goods cost aid. Also fires before-build hook.
Each time you use a wood accumulation space, +2 wood.
Places 1 clay on next 3 round spaces, collected at those rounds' start.
Places 1 reed on 5 future round spaces, collected at start of each.
Each time you plow a field (incl. playing a field card), may buy 1 stone for 1 food.
Immediately stock 2 of each resource on card; each time any player takes >=threshold of a resource, you get 1 of that resource from card.
After using Day Laborer, may pay 2 food to buy 1 of each resource.
On play, immediately gain 2 cattle (must be accommodated).
Each non-harvest round, pay 1 grain to breed exactly 1 animal type outside breeding phase; food/grain cost. Once per such round.
On play, plow 1 field completing a zigzag (geometry-constrained); passing minor.
On play, take a Renovation action; passing minor.
On play, sow in exactly 1 field; passing minor.
On play, gain wood equal to stone in supply (plain state-read); passing minor.
On play, gain clay per planted field (plain state read); passing minor.
Immediate exchange of up to 3 wood for stone; passing minor.
Gain food/grain then must sow the grain (granted sow sub-action, but mandatory here); passing.
Always-on capacity boost for size-1 pastures.
At-any-time renovate-to-stone action with a per-room cost formula; wooden-house cost includes food.
Fires before renovate-to-stone: gain goods and may take a Build Rooms action.
Before using Sheep/Cattle Market space, may build 1 stable (piece from supply) cheaply/free.
Before an unconditional Sow, may pay 3 food to plow 1 field.
After returning-home, may pay 2 wood+1 food to use Farmland without placing a person.
After using a clay accumulation space, may pay 1 clay to plow and place that clay back on the space (needs to know which space).
Each time you get a Minor Improvement action, may take Family Growth instead (provided room).

cards.new_category: PLACE-PERSON-ON-ROUND-SPACE: place a person from supply on a future round space to gain an extra usable worker that round.
cards.note: Places a person on a computed future round space to use later; count-based round offset. Closest is scheduling + people, but placing a usable PERSON on a round space isn't quite E-SCHEDANIMAL.
Card is a personal action space; renovation action rounds 3-5, choice of veg/boar/cattle rounds 6-8 (round-dependent, mandatory-choice reward).
With exactly 4 people, place 3rd and 4th person consecutively (even same space) each work phase.

cards.new_category: MULTI-IDENTITY: one card that simultaneously counts as a field, an occupation, a minor, and the Fireplace major (whichever is most convenient for another effect), never multiple at once.
cards.note: Field to sow in (L-CARDFIELD) + Fireplace baking spec (errata: no goods-to-food conversions) + flexible identity for other effects (EXOTIC/new_category).
Passively lets you build Joinery/Well via a Minor Improvement action, or both via one Major action — extends eligibility of the build action.
At end of each turn you renovate (after a renovate sub-action), optionally exchange one major improvement for another — returns a component and swaps.
End-game bonus points for count of bottom-row major improvements — scoring rule in text.
End-game bonus points per grain+veg pair across supply and fields — scoring rule.
Bonus points if >=7 goods in fields before final harvest — needs a pre-harvest field-goods snapshot (ST-STORE).
Bonus points per unused space adjacent to house if stone house — geometry-dependent scoring.
Bonus if more stone rooms than people; explicitly mutually exclusive stone-house scoring (E-SCOREGRP).
Bonus points per Nth animal on farm — scoring rule.
Bonus if gained <=2 sheep from non-breeding sources and never converted sheep to food — tracks sheep provenance/conversions over game (ST-COUNTER).
Alternates clay and wild boar on remaining round spaces, collected at round starts; animals must be accommodated.
Place food on remaining round spaces up to wood in supply, collected at round starts — plain state read caps amount.
Once per round, pay 1 reed to place food on next 4 round spaces, collected at round starts.
Place 2 food on each remaining round space, collected at round starts; the 10-card prereq is a play-condition, not a mechanic.
Before start of each round, conditional on hand composition (occupations>improvements) get 1 food; start-of-round hook.
Card is a shared action space giving 1 food; before returning-home the placed person can reuse an unoccupied space (worker reuse from this card).
In returning-home phase each round, if clay>wood get 1 food; plain state read.
Once per round skip placing second person for 1 food, place later; deferred second-placement manipulation.
Start of each work phase, if Fishing space has >=3 food get 1 food; reads board accumulation state.
Each time you use an action space on round spaces 8-11 get 1 additional food; keyed on board round-space position.
Each time you cook a sheep/boar into food, +1 food; hook on cooking conversion.
Place 1 food on each remaining round space, collected at start of those rounds.
At end of each action in which you sow vegetables, 1 food per vegetable field; after sow sub-action.

cards.new_category: FLEXIMPROV: card counts as either a minor or major improvement, whichever is convenient for another effect
cards.note: Play-cost returns a Fireplace; at-any-time convert veg/animals to food and bake grain->food. Flexible improvement-type clarification has no code.
Play-cost returns the Pottery; at-any-time clay->2 food; text scoring bonus by clay count.
At start of each harvest, if >=3 grain fields get 2 food.
On play +2 food; optional grain->food conversion during each harvest feeding phase.
Field phase: 1 food per harvested field tile orthogonally adjacent to house; farmyard geometry.
At end of each non-harvest round you may take a Bake Bread action (grain->2 food); deferrable, once per eligible round.
Field phase: if you harvest >=2 grain, get 1 extra grain; automatic, conditional on harvest amount.
On play, place grain on next 3 of the listed round spaces, collected at round start.
Each time you use Reed Bank, optionally pay 1 reed for 1 veg (4+player reed-placement branch irrelevant to 2p).
Before field phase, optionally pay 1 grain to add 1 veg to each of up to 2 veg fields (adds crops to fields).
At any time, if a field has exactly 1 good from a harvest, discard it and Sow limited to that field; needs field-state provenance.
Field phase: harvest 1 extra good from N fields where N = unfenced stables; changes field yield.
End of turn using Farm Expansion or building improvement: refund 1 wood per 2 wood paid; needs wood-paid provenance.
After feeding phase, if no food left, get 1 wood + 1 clay; automatic conditional.
Each time any player renovates to stone, you get 1 clay per newly renovated room; fires on own and opponent renovate.
After building an improvement costing >=2 clay, get 1 stone; play cost is 1 Wood OR 1 Food; needs cost provenance.
Each renovate pays 1 fewer reed (cost mod) and at end of action get 1 stone (after-hook gain).
Improvements via House Redevelopment cost 1 building resource less; fences via Farm Redevelopment cost 3 wood less; play cost may cook a boar (animal).
Each time you use Fencing space, also get 1 wild boar (must be accommodated); play cost 2 Food OR 1 Grain.
On play, build up to 3 stables free (stables are pieces from supply); passing minor.
On play, renovate to clay at no cost (cost-free renovate sub-action); passing.
Returns your placed person on Grain Utilization home to reuse this round; needs to know which space; passing.
Remove all grain from a field, gain 2 wood per grain removed; passing.
Take 2 different building resources from accumulation spaces into supply; removing from board spaces; passing.
Gain 1 of each building resource type where you already have >=4; plain conditional goods; passing.
Turn 1 grain (play cost) into 4 food; plain goods; passing.
Buy 1 vegetable for 2 food; plain goods; passing.
Up to twice, spend building resources for an animal from supply; animals must be accommodated; passing.
At end of work phase of rounds 3 and 6, move person from Farmland to an unoccupied space and take that action, or get 1 food.
Card holds animals up to number of rooms, conditional on a pasture adjacent to house (farm geometry).
Raises capacity of existing unfenced stables and pastures-with-stable.
At any time, renovate clay->stone without placing a person, paying normal cost.
Each time all other players have more people than you, take a Build Rooms action without placing a person; comparative person-count trigger keyed on others' placements.
After using a wood accumulation space, may place 1 stone (piece from supply) on it to gain a Build Fences action.
Continuous: edge-of-board fences need no wood (still uses fence pieces); depends on farmyard geometry.
Each time using Farmland/Cultivation may plow 2 instead of 1; sowing yields 1 fewer good per field (changes sow rule).
After playing a later minor, may pay 1 food to plow 1 field.
After using Cattle Market accumulation space, may pay 2 food to plow 1 field.
End of work phase, if occupying both Grain and Vegetable Seeds spaces, may plow 1 field; needs to know worker placement.
May use any Wish for Children space even if occupied.
On play stock food onto card; once per round discard 1 food from card to place an additional person that round.
End of each work phase, may sow exactly 1 crop on 1 field (granted sow sub-action).
When getting a Minor Improvement action, may build a major instead.
On play, immediately run the field phase of harvest on your farmyard only (one-off out-of-sequence field-phase harvest).
End of work phase rounds 7 and 9, may take a Sow action without placing a person.
End of work phase may bank 1 food irretrievably on card; at any time discard 6 from card to build a major at no cost.
Marks round current+3; at start of that round may play 1 occupation with no occupation cost; needs the marked round stored.
No effect beyond being a played card; prereq is a have-check. Trivial.
Continuous rule change: newborns require 2 food each feeding instead of 1 — a passive modification of the feeding requirement.
After each later improvement built/played, optionally pile reed on card for bonus points, capped by room count; kept-on-card stack + running score.
End-game bonus per farmyard column containing a room — depends on farm geometry/arrangement.
One pasture-with-stable can no longer hold animals (capacity override); plus bonus point each time you get reed from an action space.
Bonus points at scoring if farm has no unused spaces — reads farm layout.
End-game bonus based on people count — a scoring rule in text.
Continuous restriction: at least one pasture must hold no animals — an ongoing capacity/placement constraint.
On play +1 food; end-game bonus if no cattle.
Each use of Fishing space, optionally place up to 2 wood on card; end-game scoring skips 1st/4th/7th/10th wood — needs on-card count and irregular scoring.
Each harvest exchange 1 clay for 2 food or 1 bonus point; capped per harvest, converts good, may score.
Pile goods on card; each Day Laborer use get top good — on-card consumable stack drained over time.
Place food/clay on remaining even round spaces, collected at start of those rounds. 5-cards prereq is a computable play condition, no mechanic.
Place cattle/grain/cattle on next 3 round spaces; animals scheduled and must be accommodated when collected.
On play, count stables and schedule food on next N round spaces; number of stables read from farm state.
On play, place food on remaining odd-numbered round spaces (scheduled goods).
On play, place food on remaining even-numbered round spaces.
On play, place food on next 2 round spaces.
Each time you get wood from a space, schedule 1 food on next round space; keyed on obtaining wood.
In feeding phase, gain food based on house material.
After any player builds a wood room, you get 1 food; fires on own or opponent build-room sub-action.
After sowing, gain food per distinct crop type sown.
Fishing puts food on card (stack); before playing an occupation you get that amount. Two hooks: Fishing space (T-AFTER) accumulates, play-occupation (T-BEFORE) pays out.
Each room added stacks food on card; at feeding phase start you get that amount. Second hook: S-HFEED payout.
When you get boar outside breeding phase, may convert to 4 food each; keyed on obtaining boar outside breeding.
When playing/building an improvement, optionally pay 1 extra printed-cost resource to get 3 food; fires on play/build improvement.
Using Fishing, get extra food scaled to current food on the space; needs the space's amount (T-BEFORE, read before collection).
4 food placed on card; each work-phase start, if last in turn order, take 1 food from card. Depends on turn order position.
Each time you bake bread -> hook on the bake sub-action; conditional food gain from sheep/cattle counts. F-AUTO (you get).

cards.new_category: SKIP-HARVEST-PHASE: option to skip the field and breeding phases of a harvest in exchange for a flat food.
cards.note: Start-of-harvest optional choice to skip field+breeding phases for 1 food; skipping phases has no clean existing code.
Each time you use Day Laborer space -> food scaled by sheep on the Sheep Market accumulation space (reads board), capped at 4.
On play +1 food; each time you pay an occupation cost, may substitute 1 building resource for up to 2 food (cost-mod on playing an occupation).
At start of each harvest, get 4 food. Prereq (2 grain fields) is a have-check, untagged.
Once per round, may skip placing a person and take a Bake Bread action (grants a whole action without placing). 'All players have a person left' is a computable condition.
Adds a 1 grain -> 6 food bake conversion rate usable on any Bake Bread action; on build, immediately take a Bake Bread action.
Same shape as Iron Oven: 1 grain -> 3 food bake conversion rate; on build, immediately take a Bake Bread action.
On play, 1 grain per 2 completed rounds -- plain immediate goods from a state read (rounds elapsed).
Each time ANOTHER player uses Forest accumulation space, you get 1 grain -> opponent-action hook.
Each time you use Grain Seeds space, +1 grain per baking improvement owned.

cards.new_category: SOW-NONCROP: the card is a field on which a non-crop good (wood) is sown/harvested as if it were grain.
cards.note: Card-field where wood is sown/harvested like grain; on harvesting the last wood, also +1 veg.
Card-field growing only vegetables; each harvest of the last veg, may plow 1 field (granted sub-action, after harvest).
Card-field; each removal of the last grain/veg, may immediately sow the other crop on the card (granted sow + crop-swap after harvest).
Each time you sow (sub-action) adjacent to a pasture, optional extra planted good; depends on farm geometry.
Card is itself a field; during field phase if harvested >=1 good, auto get 1 food.
During field phase, optionally harvest ALL crops from one field at once (changes field-harvest rule).
On play move up to 5 fences onto card (piece pool); fences from card are free when building.
Each time you use a wood accumulation space, optionally return 1 stone for +3 wood.
On play optionally return up to 3 stables to supply, +3 wood each.
Each time you get reed/stone from an action space, +1 clay; keyed on obtaining specific goods from a space.
On play, single exchange of up to 4 building resources for equal number of other building resources.
After you sow in >=1 field, auto get 1 stone.

cards.new_category: STONE-CROP: card is a field on which STONE is sown/harvested like a crop, planted as though 3 fields but counts as 1.
cards.note: Non-standard crop type (stone) on a card-field; multi-yield-as-3 but one field. Card-field plus a novel crop mechanic.
Card is a shared action space; user gets 1 building resource of each type already owned; opponents pay you 1 food toll to use.
On play +1 food; then each time after using Day Laborer, optionally exchange 1 building resource for another.
At start of breeding phase, if you have an empty unfenced stable, get 1 sheep (must be accommodated).
Only 1 sheep required to breed sheep (relaxes 2-of-type rule); card holds 1 sheep (new holder).
